---
title: "01_data_preparation"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE) 
```

```{r}
############################################################
# 0. Load packages and create directories
############################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(haven)       # for .dta files
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(broom.mixed)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(dplyr)

# Create processed-data directory if needed
dir.create("data_processed", showWarnings = FALSE)

```

```{r}
############################################################
# 1. TRUSTWORTHINESS RATINGS (Trust_1)
############################################################

# 1.1 Load raw data
trust <- read_dta("data_raw/trustworthiness_rating.dta")

# Optional: save raw imported file as CSV for inspection
write.csv(
  trust,
  "data_processed/trustworthiness_rating_raw.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

# 1.2 Filter: users of Facebook, Instagram, or Twitter
trust_fb_ig_tw <- trust %>%
  filter(
    grepl("(^1$|^1,|,1,|,1$)", social_media) |
    grepl("(^2$|^2,|,2,|,2$)", social_media) |
    grepl("(^3$|^3,|,3,|,3$)", social_media)
  )

# 1.3 Data preparation
trust1 <- trust_fb_ig_tw %>%
  mutate(
    # Sharing outcome scaled to 0–1
    sm = (rating_headlines - 1) / 5,

    # Experimental condition
    nudge_type = case_when(
      fc_Rated   == 1 ~ "FactChecker_Rated",
      fc_Unrated == 1 ~ "Control",
      lp_Rated   == 1 ~ "Laypeople_Rated",
      lp_Unrated == 1 ~ "Control",
      trt        == 1 ~ "Control",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # Unrated conditions are treated as control
    condition_f = factor(
      nudge_type,
      levels = c("Control", "FactChecker_Rated", "Laypeople_Rated")
    ),

    real_f = factor(
      ifelse(dummy_true == 1, "Real", "Fake"),
      levels = c("Fake", "Real")
    ),

    sex_f = factor(
      ifelse(Gender == 1, "Male", "Female"),
      levels = c("Female", "Male")
    ),

    demrep_f = case_when(
      DemRep_C %in% 1:3 ~ "Democrat",
      DemRep_C %in% 4:6 ~ "Republican",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # Shift ID to avoid overlap across datasets
    id = ID + 3060,
    headline_id = factor(news_headlines)
  )

# 1.4 Keep relevant variables
vars_to_keep_trust <- c(
  "id", "Age", "sex_f", "condition_f",
  "demrep_f", "real_f", "headline_id", "sm"
)

df_trust_1 <- trust1[, vars_to_keep_trust]
df_trust_1$Experiment <- "Trust_1"

# 1.5 Save cleaned dataset
write.csv(
  df_trust_1,
  "data_processed/df_trust_1.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 2. ACCURACY NUDGES (Accuracy_1)
############################################################

# 2.1 Load datasets
s3 <- readr::read_csv("data_raw/Study_3_data.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    CRT3_1 = readr::parse_number(as.character(CRT3_1)),
    study = 3
  )

s4 <- readr::read_csv("data_raw/Study_4_data.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    CRT3_1 = readr::parse_number(as.character(CRT3_1)),
    study = 4
  )

s5 <- readr::read_csv("data_raw/Study_5_data.csv") %>%
  rename(
    Age = age,
    Sex = gender
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    CRT3_1 = readr::parse_number(as.character(CRT3_1)),
    DemRep = case_when(
      DemRep_C <= 3 ~ 1L,
      DemRep_C >= 4 ~ 2L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    study = 5
  )

# 2.2 Combine datasets
dat_raw_acc <- bind_rows(s3, s4, s5)

# 2.3 Apply filtering criteria
dat_acc <- dat_raw_acc %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(SocialMedia_Chk),
    FB == 1,
    !is.na(Condition),
    (study %in% c(3, 4) & Condition %in% c(1, 2)) |
      (study == 5 & Condition %in% c(1, 3))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    id = row_number()
  )

# 2.4 Reshape to long format
dat_long_acc <- dat_acc %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches("^(Fake|Real)[0-9]+_3$"),
    names_to = c("truth", "item_num"),
    names_pattern = "(Fake|Real)([0-9]+)_3",
    values_to = "sm_raw"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    item_num = as.integer(item_num),
    real = if_else(truth == "Real", 1L, 0L),
    real_f = factor(real, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Fake", "Real")),

    # Normalize sharing outcome to 0–1
    sm = (sm_raw - 1) / 5,
    Bsm = round(sm),

    # Harmonize treatment coding across studies
    condition_harmonized = case_when(
      study %in% c(3, 4) & Condition == 1 ~ "Control",
      study %in% c(3, 4) & Condition == 2 ~ "Accuracy",
      study == 5 & Condition == 1 ~ "Control",
      study == 5 & Condition == 3 ~ "Accuracy",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    condition_f = factor(
      condition_harmonized,
      levels = c("Control", "Accuracy")
    ),

    demrep_f = factor(
      DemRep,
      levels = c(1, 2),
      labels = c("Democrat", "Republican")
    ),

    sex_f = factor(
      Sex,
      levels = c(1, 2),
      labels = c("Male", "Female")
    ),

    study_f = factor(study),
    Experiment = paste0("accuracy_", study),
    headline_id = interaction(study, truth, item_num, drop = TRUE)
  )

vars_to_keep_acc <- c(
  "id", "Age", "sex_f", "condition_f",
  "demrep_f", "real_f", "headline_id", "sm", "Experiment"
)

df_accuracy_1 <- dat_long_acc[, vars_to_keep_acc]

# 2.5 Save cleaned dataset
write.csv(
  df_accuracy_1,
  "data_processed/df_accuracy_1.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
############################################################
# 3. FACT-CHECK LABELS (fc_label)
############################################################

# 3.1 Load raw datasets from multiple studies/platforms
fc1  <- read.csv("data_raw/connect1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "connect1")
fc2  <- read.csv("data_raw/crmturk1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "crmturk1")
fc3  <- read.csv("data_raw/forthright1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "forthright1")
fc4  <- read.csv("data_raw/lucid1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "lucid1")
fc5  <- read.csv("data_raw/prolific1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "prolific1")
fc6  <- read.csv("data_raw/prolificnr1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "prolificnr1")
fc7  <- read.csv("data_raw/regmturk1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "regmturk1")
fc8  <- read.csv("data_raw/robbmturk1_share_meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "robbmturk1")
fc9  <- read.csv("data_raw/Exp1_Share_Meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "LucidExp1")
fc10 <- read.csv("data_raw/Exp2_Share_Meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "LucidExp2")
fc11 <- read.csv("data_raw/Exp3_Share_Meta.csv", as.is = TRUE) %>% mutate(study = "MTurkExp3")

dat_raw_fc <- rbind(fc1, fc2, fc3, fc4, fc5, fc6, fc7, fc8, fc9, fc10, fc11)

# 3.2 Prepare data
df_fc_label <- dat_raw_fc %>%
  transmute(
    FM_ck = FM_ck,
    id = as.character(ID),
    Age = as.numeric(age),

    sex_f = case_when(
      female == 1 ~ "Female",
      female == 0 ~ "Male",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    condition_f = as.integer(hcondition),

    demrep_f = case_when(
      as.numeric(politics) <= 3 ~ "Democrat",
      as.numeric(politics) == 4 ~ "Independent",
      as.numeric(politics) >= 5 ~ "Republican",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    real_f = case_when(
      condition_f %in% c(1, 2, 3) ~ "Fake",
      condition_f %in% c(4, 5) ~ "Real",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    headline_id = as.character(itemID),
    sm = as.numeric(share),

    Experiment = paste0("fc_label_", study, "_", platform)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex_f = factor(sex_f),
    demrep_f = factor(
      demrep_f,
      levels = c("Democrat", "Independent", "Republican")
    )
  )

# 3.3 Social-media-use filter
df_fc_label$SM_filter <- ifelse(
  df_fc_label$FM_ck == TRUE |
    grepl("^1", df_fc_label$FM_ck) |
    grepl("^1$", df_fc_label$FM_ck) |
    grepl("2", df_fc_label$FM_ck) |
    grepl("4", df_fc_label$FM_ck) |
    grepl("999", df_fc_label$FM_ck),
  TRUE, FALSE
)

df_fc_label1 <- df_fc_label %>%
  filter(SM_filter == TRUE)

# 3.4 Save cleaned dataset
write.csv(
  df_fc_label1,
  "data_processed/df_fclabel_1.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
length(unique(df_fc_label1$id))
```




```{r}
############################################################
# 4. POOL ALL DATASETS
############################################################

# 4.1 Load prepared datasets
df_accuracy <- read_csv("data_processed/df_accuracy_1.csv")
df_trust    <- read_csv("data_processed/df_trust_1.csv")
df_fc_label <- read_csv("data_processed/df_fclabel_1.csv")

# 4.2 Harmonize ID and headline types
df_accuracy <- df_accuracy %>%
  mutate(
    headline_id = as.character(headline_id),
    id = as.character(id)
  )

df_trust <- df_trust %>%
  mutate(
    headline_id = as.character(headline_id),
    id = as.character(id)
  )

df_fc_label <- df_fc_label %>%
  mutate(
    headline_id = as.character(headline_id),
    id = as.character(id)
  )

# 4.3 Create experiment-specific indicators
df_accuracy <- df_accuracy %>%
  mutate(
    experiment_type = "accuracy",
    accuracy_nudge = if_else(condition_f == "Accuracy", 1L, 0L),
    rating_shown = 0L,
    rater_type = NA_character_,
    label_shown = 0L
  )

df_trust <- df_trust %>%
  mutate(
    experiment_type = "source_rating",
    accuracy_nudge = 0L,
    rating_shown = if_else(
      condition_f %in% c("FactChecker_Rated", "Laypeople_Rated"),
      1L, 0L
    ),
    rater_type = case_when(
      condition_f == "FactChecker_Rated" ~ "factchecker",
      condition_f == "Laypeople_Rated" ~ "laypeople",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    label_shown = 0L
  )

df_fc_label <- df_fc_label %>%
  mutate(
    experiment_type = "factcheck_label",
    accuracy_nudge = 0L,
    rating_shown = 0L,
    rater_type = NA_character_,
    condition_f = as.character(condition_f),
    label_shown = if_else(condition_f == "2", 1L, 0L)
  )

# 4.4 Pool all datasets
df_all <- bind_rows(df_accuracy, df_trust, df_fc_label)

df_all <- df_all %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(Age),
    gender = factor(sex_f),
    ideology = factor(demrep_f, levels = c("Democrat", "Independent", "Republican")),
    veracity = if_else(real_f == "Real", "Real", "Fake"),
    veracity = factor(veracity),
    study_id = factor(Experiment),
    item_id = factor(headline_id),
    participant_id = factor(id),
    share_intent = sm
  ) %>%
  group_by(study_id) %>%
  mutate(
    share_z = as.numeric(scale(sm))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 4.5 Save final pooled dataset
write.csv(
  df_all,
  "data_processed/df_all_pooled.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

############################################################
# 5. FINAL FILTERING: ANALYTICAL SAMPLE (FALSE HEADLINES)
############################################################

# This step creates the final analytical sample used for
# descriptive checks and subsequent regression analyses.
#
# Applied restrictions:
# 1. Keep only respondents in a plausible adult age range (18–75)
# 2. Restrict the sample to false headlines only
# 3. Create a standardized age variable for interaction analyses
# 4. Harmonize gender coding

# Restrict to plausible adult age range
df_all <- df_all %>%
  mutate(age = as.numeric(age)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age), age >= 18, age <= 75)

# Keep only false headlines and create analysis variables
df_fake <- df_all %>%
  filter(veracity == "Fake") %>%
  mutate(
    # Standardized age variable used in moderation analyses
    age_z = as.numeric(scale(age)),

    # Harmonize gender coding across pooled datasets
    gender = factor(gender, levels = c("Female", "Male"))
  )

# Save final analytical dataset
write.csv(
  df_fake,
  "data_processed/df_fake.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
